member.summary: Weapon Slots

member.summary: Connection statuses

member.summary: Player GUI dialog

member.name: T:__DOWNLOAD_REQUEST
member.summary: Artwork/NetModels

member.name: T:__HTTP_ERROR
member.summary: HTTP error response codes
member.remarks: These codes compliment ordinary HTTP response codes returned in 'responseCode' (10x) (20x OK) (30x Moved) (40x Unauthorised) (50x Server Error)

member.summary: HTTP request types

member.name: T:__KEY
member.summary: Keys

member.summary: Marker modes used by ShowPlayerMarkers()

member.name: T:floatround_method
member.summary: Different methods of rounding.

member.name: C:__namemax
member.summary: The maximum length of symbol names, excluding any NULLs.

member.name: F:__OPEN_MP_VERSION
member.remarks: If running on SA:MP this will remain 0, open.mp will set it. open.mp versions look like: 01.04.02.0544 Thus they are encoded here as: (major << 28) | (minor << 21) | (patch << 14) | (prerel)

member.name: F:__SAMP_INCLUDES_VERSION
member.remarks:
Version examples:
0.3.DL R1 - 03D010
0.3.7 R3 - 037030
0.3.7 R2-2 - 037022
0.3.7 R1-2 - 037012
0.3.7 - 037000
0.3z R4 - 030700
0.3z R3 - 030700
0.3z R2-1 - 030700
0.3z R1-2 - 030700
0.3z - 030700
0.3x R2 patch 1 - 030621
0.3x R2 - 030620
0.3x R1-2 - 030612
0.3x - 030600
0.3e - 030500
0.3d - 030400
0.3c - 030300
0.3b - 030200
0.3a - 030100
0.2X - 02A000
0.2.2 R3 - 022300 Rough rules:
Uses (roughtly) BCD. Special versions are denoted outside 0-9. 0.1.2c R4-5 | | || | | 0 1 23 4 5 = 0x012345 (assuming c is the third revision)
0.2X becomes 02A000 because it is basically 0.2.3, but not, while higher than 0.2.2 so can't be 020400 (for example). Also, its a capital letter, so doesn't use the revision method.
P.S. Making a consistent scheme for SA:MP versions is REALLY hard.
open.mp releases can use `A` as the first digit.

member.name: M:@timer
member.summary: A timer event occurred.
member.remarks:
This function executes after the configured delay or interval expires. See settimer to set the delay or interval. Depending on the timing precision of the host, the call may occur later than the delay that was set.
If the timer was set as a "single-shot", it must be ex plicitly set again for a next execution for the @timerfunction. If the timer is set to be repetitive, @timer will continue to be called with the set interval until it is disabled with another call to settimer.
member.returns: The return value of this function is currently ignored.

member.name: M:AddPlayerClass
member.syntax: AddPlayerClass(skin, spawnX, spawnY, spawnZ, angle, weapon1, ammo1, weapon2, ammo2, weapon3, ammo3)
member.param: The skin which the player will spawn with
Float The x coordinate of the spawnpoint of this class
Float The y coordinate of the spawnpoint of this class
Float The z coordinate of the spawnpoint of this class
Float The direction in which the player should face after spawning
t_WEAPON The first spawn-weapon for the player
The amount of ammunition for the primary spawn weapon
t_WEAPON The second spawn-weapon for the player
The amount of ammunition for the second spawn weapon
t_WEAPON The third spawn-weapon for the player
The amount of ammunition for the third spawn weapon
member.summary: Adds a class to class selection. Classes are used so players may spawn with a skin of their choice.

ul.li: The ID of the class which was just added.
319 if the class limit (320) was reached. The highest possible class ID is 319.

member.remarks: The maximum class ID is 319 (starting from 0, so a total of 320 classes). When this limit is reached, any more classes that are added will replace ID 319.

member.name: M:CreatePlayerTextDraw
member.param: The ID of the player to create the textdraw for
Float x-Coordinate
Float y-Coordinate
[] The text in the textdraw. May be optionally formatted.
member.summary: Creates a textdraw for a single player. This can be used as a way around the global text-draw limit.
member.remarks:
If you choose values for y that are less than 1, the first text row will be invisible and only the shadow is visible.
If part of the text is off-screen, the colour of the text will not show, only the shadow (if enabled) will.

Maximum length of textdraw is 800 characters.
If you use colour codes (such as ~R~ ~G~) beyond 255th character the legacy (SA:MP) client will crash trying to display the textdraw.
Keyboard key mapping codes (such as ~k~~VEHICLE_ENTER_EXIT~ Doesn't work beyond 255th character.

The x, y coordinate is the top left coordinate for the text draw area based on a 640x480 "canvas" (irrespective of screen resolution). If you plan on using PlayerTextDrawAlignment with alignment 3 (right), the x, y coordinate is the top right coordinate for the text draw.
This function merely CREATES the textdraw, you must use PlayerTextDrawShow to show it to a player.
It is recommended to use WHOLE numbers instead of decimal positions when creating player textdraws to ensure resolution friendly design.
Player-textdraws are automatically destroyed when a player disconnects.
member.returns: The ID of the created textdraw.

member.name: M:EnableTirePopping
member.param: bool 1 to enable, 0 to disable tire popping
member.remarks: Tire popping is enabled by default. If you want to disable tire popping, you'll have to manually script it using OnVehicleDamageStatusUpdate.

member.name: M:GetActorPoolSize
member.summary: Gets the highest actorid created on the server.
member.returns: The highest actorid created on the server or 0 if there are no created actors.

member.name: M:GetPlayerName
member.param: The ID of the player to get the name of
[] An array into which to store the name, passed by reference
The length of the string that should be stored. Recommended to be MAX_PLAYER_NAME
member.summary: Get a player's name.
member.remarks: A player's name can be up to 24 characters long (as of 0.3d R2) by using SetPlayerName. This is defined as 24 by default. However, the client can only join with a nickname between 3 and 20 characters, otherwise the connection will be rejected and the player has to quit to choose a valid name.
member.returns: The length of the player's name. 0 if player specified doesn't exist.

member.name: M:GetPlayerPoolSize
member.summary: Gets the highest playerid currently in use on the server.
member.returns: The highest playerid currently in use on the server or 0 if there are no connected players.

member.name: M:GetTickCount
member.summary: Returns the uptime of the actual server (not the open.mp server) in milliseconds.
member.remarks: GetTickCount will cause problems on servers with uptime of over 24 days as GetTickCount will eventually warp past the integer size constraints. However using this function fixes the problem.
One common use for GetTickCount is for benchmarking. It can be used to calculate how much time some code takes to execute.
member.returns: Uptime of the actual server (not the open.mp server).

member.name: M:GetVehiclePoolSize
member.summary: Gets the highest vehicleid currently in use on the server.
member.returns: The highest vehicleid currently in use on the server or 0 if there are no created vehicles.

member.name: M:HTTP
member.param: ID used to differentiate requests that are sent to the same callback (useful for playerids)
t_HTTP_METHOD The type of request you wish to send
[] The URL you want to request. (Without 'http://')
[] Any POST data you want to send with the request
[] Name of the callback function you want to use to handle responses to this request
member.summary: Sends a threaded HTTP request.
member.returns: 1 on success, 0 on failure.

example.code:
// HTTP callback.
public MyHttpResponse(index, responseCode, const data[]) { ... }

member.name: M:IsPlayerConnected
member.param: The ID of the player to check
member.summary: Checks if a player is connected (if an ID is taken by a connected player).
member.remarks: This function can be omitted in a lot of cases. Many other functions already have some sort of connection check built in.
member.returns: 1 if the player is connected, 0 if not.

member.name: M:Kick
member.param: The ID of the player to kick
member.summary: Kicks a player from the server. They will have to quit the game and re-connect if they wish to continue playing.
member.remarks: Any action taken directly before Kick() (such as sending a message with SendClientMessage) will not reach the player. A timer must be used to delay the kick.
member.returns: This function always returns 1, even if the function failed to execute (player specified doesn't exist).

member.name: M:KillTimer
member.param: The ID of the timer to kill (returned by SetTimer or SetTimerEx)
member.summary: Kills (stops) a running timer.
member.returns: This function always returns 0.

member.name: M:OnActorStreamIn
member.param: The ID of the actor that has been streamed in for the player
The ID of the player that streamed the actor in
member.summary: This callback is called when an actor is streamed in by a player's client.
member.remarks: This callback can also be called by NPC.
It is always called first in filterscripts.
member.returns: This callback does not handle returns.

member.name: M:OnActorStreamOut
member.param: The ID of the actor that has been streamed out for the player
The ID of the player that streamed the actor out
member.summary: This callback is called when an actor is streamed out by a player's client.
member.remarks: This callback can also be called by NPC.
It is always called first in filterscripts.
member.returns: This callback does not handle returns.

member.name: M:OnClientCheckResponse
member.param: The ID of the player checked
The type of check performed
The address requested
The result of the check
member.summary: This callback is called when a SendClientCheck request comletes

member.name: M:OnDialogResponse
member.param: The ID of the player that responded to the dialog
The ID of the dialog the player responded to, assigned in ShowPlayerDialog
1 for left button and 0 for right button (if only one button shown, always 1)
The ID of the list item selected by the player (starts at 0) (only if using a list style dialog)
[] The text entered into the input box by the player or the selected list item text
member.summary: This callback is called when a player responds to a dialog shown using ShowPlayerDialog by either clicking a button, pressing ENTER/ESC or double-clicking a list item (if using a list style dialog).
member.remarks: A player's dialog doesn't hide when the gamemode restarts, causing the server to print "Warning: PlayerDialogResponse PlayerId: 0 dialog ID doesn't match last sent dialog ID" if a player responded to this dialog after restart.
Parameters can contain different values, based on dialog's style.
member.returns:
Returning 0 in this callback will pass the dialog to another script in case no matching code were found in your gamemode's callback.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 1 there blocks other filterscripts from seeing it.

member.name: M:OnEnterExitModShop
member.param: The ID of the player that entered or exited the modshop
1 if the player entered or 0 if they exited
The interior ID of the modshop that the player is entering (or 0 if exiting)
member.summary: This callback is called when a player enters or exits a mod shop.
member.remarks: Players collide when they get into the same mod shop.
member.returns:
This callback does not handle returns.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

member.name: M:OnFilterScriptExit
member.summary: This callback is called when a filterscript is unloaded. It is only called inside the filterscript which is unloaded.
member.returns: This callback does not handle returns.

member.name: M:OnFilterScriptInit
member.summary: This callback is called when a filterscript is initialized (loaded). It is only called inside the filterscript which is starting.
member.returns: This callback does not handle returns.

member.name: M:OnGameModeExit
member.summary: This callback is called when a gamemode ends, either through 'gmx', the server being shut down, or GameModeExit.
member.remarks: This function can also be used in a filterscript to detect if the gamemode changes with RCON commands like changemode or gmx, as changing the gamemode does not reload a filterscript.

When using OnGameModeExit in conjunction with the 'rcon gmx' console command keep in mind there is a potential for client bugs to occur an example of this is excessive RemoveBuildingForPlayer calls during OnGameModeInit which could result in a client crash.
This callback will NOT be called if the server crashes or the process is killed by other means, such as using the Linux kill command or pressing the close-button on the Windows console.
member.returns:
0 - Will prevent other filterscripts from receiving this callback.
1 - Indicates that this callback will be passed to the next filterscript.
It is always called first in gamemode.

member.name: M:OnGameModeInit
member.summary: This callback is triggered when the gamemode starts.
member.remarks: This function can also be used in a filterscript to detect if the gamemode changes with RCON commands like changemode or gmx, as changing the gamemode does not reload a filterscript.
member.returns:
0 - Will prevent other filterscripts from receiving this callback.
1 - Indicates that this callback will be passed to the next filterscript.
It is always called first in gamemode.

member.name: M:OnIncomingConnection
member.param: The ID of the player attempting to connect
[] The IP address of the player attempting to connect
The port of the attempted connection
member.summary: This callback is called when an IP address attempts a connection to the server. To block incoming connections, use BlockIpAddress.
member.returns:
1 - Will prevent other filterscripts from receiving this callback.
0 - Indicates that this callback will be passed to the next filterscript.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

member.name: M:OnNPCWeaponShot
member.syntax: OnNPCWeaponShot(npcid, weaponid, hittype, hitid, fX, fY, fZ)

member.name: M:OnObjectMoved
member.param: The ID of the object that was moved
member.summary: This callback is called when an object is moved after MoveObject (when it stops moving).
member.remarks: SetObjectPos does not work when used in this callback. To fix it, recreate the object.
member.returns:
This callback does not handle returns.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

member.name: M:OnPickupStreamIn
member.param: The ID of the pickup, returned by CreatePickup
The ID of the player that picked up the pickup
member.summary: Called when a pickup enters the visual range of a player.
member.returns:
This callback does not handle returns.
It is always called first in gamemode.

member.name: M:OnPickupStreamOut
member.param: The ID of the pickup, returned by CreatePickup
The ID of the player that picked up the pickup
member.summary: Called when a pickup leaves the visual range of a player.
member.returns:
This callback does not handle returns.
It is always called first in gamemode.

member.name: M:OnPlayerClickGangZone
member.param: The ID of the player that clicked a gangzone
The ID of the gangzone the player clicked
member.summary: This callback is called when a player clicked a gangzone.
member.returns:
This callback does not handle returns.
It is always called first in gamemode.

member.name: M:OnPlayerClickMap
member.param: The ID of the player that placed a target/waypoint
Float The X float coordinate where the player clicked
Float The Y float coordinate where the player clicked
Float The Z float coordinate where the player clicked (inaccurate - see note below)
member.summary: OnPlayerClickMap is called when a player places a target/waypoint on the pause menu map (by right-clicking).
member.remarks: The Z value returned will be 0 (invalid) if it is far away from the player; use the MapAndreas plugin to get a more accurate Z coordinate.
member.returns:
1 - Will prevent other filterscripts from receiving this callback.
0 - Indicates that this callback will be passed to the next filterscript.
It is always called first in gamemode.

member.name: M:OnPlayerClickPlayer
member.param: The ID of the player that clicked on a player on the scoreboard
The ID of the player that was clicked on
t_CLICK_SOURCE The source of the player's click
member.summary: Called when a player double-clicks on a player on the scoreboard.
member.remarks: There is currently only one (0 - CLICK_SOURCE_SCOREBOARD). The existence of this argument suggests that more sources may be supported in the future.
member.returns:
1 - Will prevent other filterscripts from receiving this callback.
0 - Indicates that this callback will be passed to the next filterscript.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

member.name: M:OnPlayerClickPlayerGangZone
member.param: The ID of the player that clicked a player gangzone
The ID of the player gangzone the player clicked
member.summary: This callback is called when a player clicked a player gangzone.
member.returns:
This callback does not handle returns.
It is always called first in gamemode.

member.name: M:OnPlayerClickPlayerTextDraw
member.param: The ID of the player that selected a textdraw
PlayerText The ID of the player-textdraw that the player selected
member.summary: This callback is called when a player clicks on a player-textdraw. It is not called when player cancels the select mode (ESC) - however, OnPlayerClickTextDraw is.
member.remarks: When a player presses ESC to cancel selecting a textdraw, OnPlayerClickTextDraw is called with a textdraw ID of INVALID_TEXT_DRAW. OnPlayerClickPlayerTextDraw won't be called also.
member.returns:
Returning 1 in this callback will prevent it being called in other scripts. This should be used to signal that the textdraw on which they clicked was 'found' and no further processing is needed. You should return 0 if the textdraw on which they clicked wasn't found, just like in OnPlayerCommandText.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 1 there also blocks other scripts from seeing it.

member.name: M:OnPlayerClickTextDraw
member.param: The ID of the player that clicked on the textdraw
Text The ID of the clicked textdraw. INVALID_TEXT_DRAW if selection was cancelled
member.summary: This callback is called when a player clicks on a textdraw or cancels the select mode with the Escape key.
member.remarks: The clickable area is defined by TextDrawTextSize. The x and y parameters passed to that function must not be zero or negative.
Do not use CancelSelectTextDraw unconditionally within this callback. This results in an infinite loop.
member.returns:
Returning 1 in this callback will prevent it being called in other scripts. This should be used to signal that the textdraw on which they clicked was 'found' and no further processing is needed. You should return 0 if the textdraw on which they clicked wasn't found, just like in OnPlayerCommandText.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 1 there also blocks other scripts from seeing it.

member.name: M:OnPlayerCommandText
member.param: The ID of the player that entered a command
[] The command that was entered (including the forward slash)
member.summary: This callback is called when a player enters a command into the client chat window. Commands are anything that start with a forward slash, e.g. /help.
member.remarks: This callback can also be called by NPC.
member.returns:
Return 1 if the command was processed, otherwise 0; If the command was not found both in filterscripts and in gamemode, the player will be received a message: SERVER: Unknown command.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 1 there blocks other scripts from seeing it.

member.name: M:OnPlayerConnect
member.param: The ID of the player that connected
member.summary: This callback is called when a player connects to the server.
member.remarks: This callback can also be called by NPC.
member.returns:
0 - Will prevent other filterscripts from receiving this callback.
1 - Indicates that this callback will be passed to the next filterscript.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

member.name: M:OnPlayerDeath
member.param: The ID of the player that died
The ID of the player that killed the player who died, or INVALID_PLAYER_ID if there was none
t_WEAPON The ID of the reason for the player's death
member.summary: This callback is called when a player dies, either by suicide or by being killed by another player.
member.remarks:
The reason will return 37 (flame thrower) from any fire sources (e.g. molotov, 18)
The reason will return 51 from any weapon that creates an explosion (e.g. RPG, grenade)
You do not need to check whether killerid is valid before using it in SendDeathMessage. INVALID_PLAYER_ID is a valid killerid ID parameter in that function.
playerid is the only one who can call the callback. (good to know for anti fake death)
member.returns:
0 - Will prevent other filterscripts from receiving this callback.
1 - Indicates that this callback will be passed to the next filterscript.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

member.name: M:OnPlayerDisconnect
member.param: The ID of the player that disconnected
The reason for the disconnection. See table below
member.summary: This callback is called when a player disconnects from the server.
member.remarks: This callback can also be called by NPC.

Some functions might not work correctly when used in this callback because the player is already disconnected when the callback is called. This means that you can't get unambiguous information from functions like GetPlayerIp and GetPlayerPos.

ul.li: 0 - timeout/Crash - the player's connection was lost. Either their game crashed or their network had a fault.
1 - quit - the player purposefully quit, either using the /quit (/q) command or via the pause menu.
2 - kick/ban - the player was kicked or banned by the server.

member.returns:
0 - Will prevent other filterscripts from receiving this callback.
1 - Indicates that this callback will be passed to the next filterscript.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

member.name: M:OnPlayerEditAttachedObject

t_EDIT_RESPONSE 0 if they cancelled (ESC) or 1 if they clicked the save icon

The bone of the attached object that was edited

member.param: The ID of the player that finished downloading custom models
The ID of the virtual world the player finished downloading custom models for
member.summary: This callback is called when a player finishes downloading custom models. For more information on how to add custom models to your server, see the release thread and this tutorial.
member.remarks: This callback is called every time a player changes virtual worlds, even if there are no custom models present in that world.
member.returns: This callback does not handle returns.

member.name: M:OnPlayerGiveDamage
member.param: The ID of the player that gave damage
The ID of the player that received damage
Float The amount of health/armour damagedid has lost (combined)
t_WEAPON The reason that caused the damage
The body part that was hit.
member.summary: This callback is called when a player gives damage to another player.
member.remarks:
Keep in mind this function can be inaccurate in some cases.
If you want to prevent certain players from damaging eachother, use SetPlayerTeam.
The weaponid will return 37 (flame thrower) from any fire sources (e.g. molotov, 18)
The weaponid will return 51 from any weapon that creates an explosion (e.g. RPG, grenade)
playerid is the only one who can call the callback.
The amount is always the maximum damage the weaponid can do, even when the health left is less than that maximum damage. So when a player has 100.0 health and gets shot with a Desert Eagle which has a damage value of 46.2, it takes 3 shots to kill that player. All 3 shots will show an amount of 46.2, even though when the last shot hits, the player only has 7.6 health left.
member.returns:
1 - Callback will not be called in other filterscripts.
0 - Allows this callback to be called in other filterscripts.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 1 there blocks other filterscripts from seeing it.

member.name: M:OnPlayerGiveDamageActor

The ID of the actor that received damage
Float The amount of health/armour damaged_actorid has lost
t_WEAPON The reason that caused the damage
The body part that was hit
member.summary: This callback is called when a player gives damage to an actor.
member.remarks: This function does not get called if the actor is set invulnerable (WHICH IS BY DEFAULT). See SetActorInvulnerable.
member.returns:
1 - Callback will not be called in other filterscripts.
0 - Allows this callback to be called in other filterscripts.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 1 there blocks other filterscripts from seeing it.

t_PLAYER_STATE The player's previous state

member.remarks: This callback can also be called by NPC.

ul.li: PLAYER_STATE_DRIVER - player is the driver of a vehicle
PLAYER_STATE_PASSENGER - player is passenger of a vehicle
PLAYER_STATE_WASTED - player is dead or on class selection
PLAYER_STATE_ENTER_VEHICLE_DRIVER - player enters a vehicle as driver
PLAYER_STATE_ENTER_VEHICLE_PASSENGER - player enters a vehicle as passenger

member.returns:
This callback does not handle returns.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

The body part that was hit.
member.summary: This callback is called when a player takes damage.
member.remarks: GetPlayerHealth and GetPlayerArmour will return the old amounts of the player before this callback.

The weaponid will return 37 (flame thrower) from any fire sources (e.g. molotov, 18).
The weaponid will return 51 from any weapon that creates an explosion (e.g. RPG, grenade)
playerid is the only one who can call the callback.
The amount is always the maximum damage the weaponid can do, even when the health left is less than that maximum damage. So when a player has 100.0 health and gets shot with a Desert Eagle which has a damage value of 46.2, it takes 3 shots to kill that player. All 3 shots will show an amount of 46.2, even though when the last shot hits, the player only has 7.6 health left.

member.name: M:OnPlayerText
member.param: The ID of the player who typed the text
[] The text the player typed
member.summary: Called when a player sends a chat message.
member.remarks: This callback can also be called by NPC.
member.returns:
Returning 0 in this callback will stop the text from being sent to all players.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 0 there blocks other scripts from seeing it.

member.name: M:OnPlayerUpdate
member.param: ID of the player sending an update packet
member.summary: This callback is called every time a client/player updates the server with their status. It is often used to create custom callbacks for client updates that aren't actively tracked by the server, such as health or armor updates or players switching weapons.
member.remarks: This callback can also be called by NPC.
This callback is called, on average, 30 times per second, per player; only use it when you know what it's meant for (or more importantly what it's NOT meant for).
The frequency with which this callback is called for each player varies, depending on what the player is doing. Driving or shooting will trigger a lot more updates than idling.
member.returns:
0 - Update from this player will not be replicated to other clients.
1 - Indicates that this update can be processed normally and sent to other players.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

member.name: M:OnPlayerWeaponShot
member.syntax: OnPlayerWeaponShot(playerid, weaponid, hittype, hitid, fX, fY, fZ)
member.param: The ID of the player that shot a weapon
t_WEAPON The ID of the weapon shot by the player
t_BULLET_HIT_TYPE The type of thing the shot hit (none, player, vehicle, or (player)object)
The ID of the player, vehicle or object that was hit
Float The X coordinate that the shot hit
Float The Y coordinate that the shot hit
Float The Z coordinate that the shot hit
member.summary: This callback is called when a player fires a shot from a weapon. Only bullet weapons are supported. Only passenger drive-by is supported (not driver drive-by, and not sea sparrow / hunter shots).
member.remarks: BULLET_HIT_TYPE_PLAYER is also called for NPCs. Actors are ignored by this callback and detects as BULLET_HIT_TYPE_NONE.
This callback is only called when lag compensation is enabled.

Isn't called if you fired in vehicle as driver or if you are looking behind with the aim enabled (shooting in air).
It is called as BULLET_HIT_TYPE_VEHICLE with the correct hitid (the hit player's vehicleid) if you are shooting a player which is in a vehicle. It won't be called as BULLET_HIT_TYPE_PLAYER at all.
GetPlayerLastShotVectors can be used in this callback for more detailed bullet vector information.
member.returns:
0 - Prevent the bullet from causing damage.
1 - Allow the bullet to cause damage.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 0 there also blocks other scripts from seeing it.

member.name: M:OnRconCommand
member.param: [] A string containing the command that was typed, as well as any passed parameters
member.summary: This callback is called when a command is sent through the server console, remote RCON, or via the in-game "/rcon command".
member.remarks: You will need to include this callback in a loaded filterscript for it to work in the gamemode!
"/rcon" is not included in "cmd" when a player types a command.
If you use the print function here, it will send a message to the player who typed the command in-game as well as the log.
This callback is not called when the player is not logged in as RCON admin.

When the player is not logged in as RCON admin and uses /rcon login, this callback will not be called and OnRconLoginAttempt is called instead. However, when the player is logged in as RCON admin, the use of this command will call this callback.
member.returns:
0 if the command was not processed, it will be passed to another script or 1 if the command was processed, will not be passed to other scripts.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 1 there blocks gamemode from seeing it.

member.name: M:OnRconLoginAttempt
member.param: [] The IP of the player that tried to log in to RCON
[] The password used to login with
0 if the password was incorrect or 1 if it was correct
member.summary: This callback is called when someone attempts to log in to RCON in-game; successful or not.
member.remarks: This callback is only called when /rcon login is used in-game.
This callback is only called when the player is not yet logged in. When the player is logged in, OnRconCommand is called instead.
member.returns:
This callback does not handle returns.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

member.name: M:OnScriptLoadPlayer
member.summary: This callback is called for each connected player when a script is loaded.
member.returns: This callback does not handle returns.

member.name: M:OnScriptUnloadPlayer
member.summary: This callback is called for each connected player when a script is unloaded.
member.returns: This callback does not handle returns.

member.name: M:OnTrailerUpdate
member.param: The ID of the player who sent a trailer update
The Trailer being updated
member.summary: This callback is called when a player sent a trailer update.
member.remarks: This callback is called very frequently per second per trailer. You should refrain from implementing intensive calculations or intensive file writing/reading operations in this callback.
member.returns:
0 - Cancels any trailer updates from being sent to other players. Update is still sent to the updating player.
1 - Processes the trailer update as normal and synchronizes it between all players.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

member.name: M:OnUnoccupiedVehicleUpdate
member.syntax: OnUnoccupiedVehicleUpdate(vehicleid, playerid, passenger_seat, new_x, new_y, new_z, vel_x, vel_y, vel_z)
member.param: The ID of the vehicle that's position was updated
The ID of the player that sent a vehicle position sync update
The ID of the seat if the player is a passenger. 0=not in vehicle, 1=front passenger, 2=backleft 3=backright 4+ is for coach/bus etc. with many passenger seats
Float The new X coordinate of the vehicle.
Float The new Y coordinate of the vehicle.
Float The new Z coordinate of the vehicle.
Float The new X velocity of the vehicle.
Float The new Y velocity of the vehicle.
Float The new Z velocity of the vehicle.
member.summary: This callback is called when a player's client updates/syncs the position of a vehicle they're not driving. This can happen outside of the vehicle or when the player is a passenger of a vehicle that has no driver.
member.remarks: This callback is called very frequently per second per unoccupied vehicle. You should refrain from implementing intensive calculations or intensive file writing/reading operations in this callback.
GetVehiclePos will return the old coordinates of the vehicle before this update.
member.returns:
Returning 0 in this callback will stop the vehicle's position being synced to other players. Update is still sent to the updating player. Useful for combating vehicle teleport hacks.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 0 there also blocks other scripts from seeing it.

member.name: M:OnVehicleDamageStatusUpdate
member.param: The ID of the vehicle that was changed its damage status
The ID of the player who synced the change in the damage status (who had the car damaged or repaired)
member.summary: This callback is called when a vehicle element such as doors, tires, panels, or lights change their damage status.
member.remarks: This does not include vehicle health changes
member.returns:
1 - Will prevent other filterscripts from receiving this callback.
0 - Indicates that this callback will be passed to the next filterscript.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

member.name: M:OnVehicleDeath
member.param: The ID of the vehicle that was destroyed
The ID of the player that reported (synced) the vehicle's destruction (name is misleading). Generally the driver or a passenger (if any) or the closest player
member.summary: This callback is called when a vehicle is destroyed - either by exploding or becoming submerged in water.
member.remarks: This callback can also be called by NPC.

This callback will also be called when a vehicle enters water, but the vehicle can be saved from destruction by teleportation or driving out (if only partially submerged). The callback won't be called a second time, and the vehicle may disappear when the driver exits, or after a short time.
member.returns:
This callback does not handle returns.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

The ID of the component which was added to the vehicle
member.summary: This callback is called when a vehicle is modded.
member.remarks: This callback is NOT called by AddVehicleComponent.
member.returns:

It is always called first in gamemode so returning 0 there also blocks other filterscripts from seeing it.

member.name: M:OnVehicleRespray

The ID of the vehicle that was resprayed

The colour that the vehicle's secondary colour was changed to
member.summary: This callback is called when a player exits a mod shop, even if the colours weren't changed. Watch out, the name is ambiguous, Pay 'n' Spray shops don't call this callback.

This callback is not called by ChangeVehicleColor.
member.returns:
Returning 0 in this callback will deny the colour change. Returning 1 will allow it. This can be used to prevent hackers from changing vehicle colours using cheats.
It is always called first in gamemode so returning 0 there also blocks other filterscripts from seeing it.

member.name: M:OnVehicleSirenStateChange
member.param: The ID of the player that toggled the siren (driver)
The ID of the vehicle of which the siren was toggled for
0 if siren was turned off, 1 if siren was turned on
member.summary: This callback is called when a vehicle's siren is toggled.
member.remarks: This callback can also be called by NPC.
This callback is only called when a vehicle's siren is toggled on or off, NOT when the alternate siren is in use (holding horn).
member.returns:
1 - Will prevent gamemode from receiving this callback.
0 - Indicates that this callback will be passed to the gamemode.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

member.name: M:OnVehicleSpawn

member.summary: This callback is called when a vehicle respawns.
member.returns:
0 - Will prevent other filterscripts from receiving this callback.
1 - Indicates that this callback will be passed to the next filterscript.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

This callback does not handle returns.

member.name: M:PlayerTextDrawSetShadow

PlayerText The ID of the textdraw to show
The size of the shadow. 0 will hide the shadow
member.summary: Show a player-textdraw to the player it was created for.

member.name: M:PlayerTextDrawTextSize
member.param: The ID of the player whose player-textdraw to set the size of
PlayerText The ID of the player-textdraw to set the size of
Float The size on the x axis (left/right) following the same 640x480 grid as TextDrawCreate
Float The size on the y axis (up/down) following the same 640x480 grid as TextDrawCreate
member.summary: Change the size of a player-textdraw (box if PlayerTextDrawUseBox is enabled and/or clickable area for use with PlayerTextDrawSetSelectable).
member.remarks:
The x and y have different meanings with different PlayerTextDrawAlignment values:
1 (left): they are the right-most corner of the box, absolute coordinates.
2 (center): they need to inverted (switch the two) and the x value is the overall width of the box.
3 (right): the x and y are the coordinates of the left-most corner of the box

Using font type 4 (sprite) and 5 (model preview) converts x and y of this function from corner coordinates to WIDTH and HEIGHT (offsets).
The TextDraw box starts 10.0 units up and 5.0 to the left as the origin (TextDrawCreate coordinate).
This function defines the clickable area for use with PlayerTextDrawSetSelectable, whether a box is shown or not.

member.name: M:SHA256_PassHash
member.param: [] The password to hash
[] The salt to use in the hash
[] The returned hash
The returned hash maximum length
member.summary: Hashes a password using the SHA-256 hashing algorithm. Includes a salt. The output is always 256 bytes in length, or the equivalent of 64 Pawn cells.
member.remarks: The salt is appended to the end of the password, meaning password 'foo' and salt 'bar' would form 'foobar'.
The salt should be random, unique for each player and at least as long as the hashed password. It is to be stored alongside the actual hash in the player's account.
The result is 64 charaters long, so the output array must be at least 65 cells big.

member.name: M:SendClientMessage
member.param: The ID of the player to display the message to
The colour of the message (RGBA)
[] The text that will be displayed (max 144 characters). Optionally formatted.
member.summary: This function sends a message to a specific player with a chosen colour in the chat. The whole line in the chatbox will be in the set colour unless colour embedding is used (since 0.3c).
member.remarks: If a message is longer than 144 characters, it will not be sent. Truncation can be used to prevent this. Displaying a message on multiple lines will also solve this issue.
Avoid using the percent sign (or format specifiers) in the actual message text without properly escaping it (like %%). It will result in crashes otherwise.
You can use colour embedding for multiple colours in the message.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully. Success is reported when the string is over 144 characters, but the message won't be sent.
0: The function failed to execute. The player is not connected.

member.name: M:SendRconCommand
member.param: [] The RCON command to be executed. May be optionally formatted.
member.summary: Sends an RCON (Remote Console) command.
member.remarks: Does not support login, due to the lack of a 'playerid' parameter.
'password 0' will remove the server's password if one is set.
member.returns: This function always returns 1.
member.remarks: This function will result in OnRconCommand being called.

member.name: M:SetDeathDropAmount

member.name: M:SetTeamCount
member.param: Number of teams the gamemode knows
member.summary: This function is used to change the amount of teams used in the gamemode. It has no obvious way of being used, but can help to indicate the number of teams used for better (more effective) internal handling. This function should only be used in the OnGameModeInit callback. Important: You can pass 2 billion here if you like, this function has no effect at all.

member.name: M:SetTimerEx
member.param: [] The name of a public function to call when the timer expires
Interval in milliseconds
bool Whether the timer should be called repeatedly (can only be stopped with KillTimer) or only once
[] Special format indicating the types of values the timer will pass

{Bit,Bitmap,XML,XMLEntry,Group,INI,Language,Style,DB,DBResult,Menu,Text,PlayerText,Text3D,PlayerText3D,File,Float,CUSTOM_TAG_TYPES,_} Indefinite number of arguments to pass (must follow format specified in previous parameter)
member.summary: Sets a timer to call a function after the specified interval. This variant ('Ex') can pass parameters (such as a player ID) to the function.
member.remarks: Timer IDs are never used twice. You can use KillTimer() on a timer ID and it won't matter if it's running or not.
The function that should be called must be public.
The use of many timers will result in increased memory/cpu usage.

ul.li: a - array The next parameter must be an integer ("i") with the array's size

member.returns: The ID of the timer that was started. Timer IDs start at 1 and are never reused. There are no internal checks to verify that the parameters passed are valid (e.g. duration not a minus value).

member.name: M:__printf
member.param: [] The format string
{Bit,Bitmap,XML,XMLEntry,Group,INI,Language,Style,DB,DBResult,Menu,Text,PlayerText,Text3D,PlayerText3D,File,Float,CUSTOM_TAG_TYPES,_} Indefinite number of arguments of any tag
member.summary: Outputs a formatted string on the console (the server window, not the in-game chat).
member.remarks: The format string or its output should not exceed 1024 characters. Anything beyond that length can lead to a server to crash.
This function doesn't support packed strings.

ul.li: %q - escape a text for SQLite. (Added in 0.3.7 R2)

member.remarks: The values for the placeholders follow in the exact same order as parameters in the call. For example, "I am %i years old" - the %i will be replaced with an Integer variable, which is the person's age.

You may optionally put a number between the % and the letter of the placeholder code. This number indicates the field width; if the size of the parameter to print at the position of the placeholder is smaller than the field width, the field is expanded with spaces. To cut the number of decimal places beeing shown of a float, you can add .<max number> between the % and the f. (example: %.2f)

member.name: M:clreol
member.summary: Clear the rest of the line.
member.remarks: Clears the line at which the cursor is, from the position of the cursor to the right margin of the console. This function does not move the cursor.

member.name: M:clrscr
member.summary: Clear the screen.
member.remarks: Clears the display and moves the cursor to the upper left corner.

member.name: M:cvttimestamp
member.summary: Convert a time-stamp into a date and time.
member.param: This will hold the year upon return.
This will hold the month (1-12) upon return.
This will hold the day of (1-31) the month upon return.
This will hold the hour (0-23) upon return.
This will hold the minute (0-59) upon return.
This will hold the second (0-59) upon return.
member.remarks: Some file and system functions return time-stamps as the number of seconds since midnight, 1 January 1970, which is the start of the UNIX system epoch. This function allows to convert these time stamps into date and time fields. cvttimestamp = ConVerT TIMESTAMP.
member.returns: This function always returns 0.

member.name: M:delay
member.summary: Halts execution a number of milliseconds.
member.param: The delay, in milliseconds.
member.remarks: On some platforms, the sleep instruction also delays for a given number of milliseconds. The difference between the sleep instruction and the delay function is that the delay function does not yield events and the sleep instruction typically yields. When yielding events, any pending events are handled. As a result, the delay function waits without handling any pending events and the sleep instruction waits and deals with events.
member.returns: This function always returns 0.

member.name: M:diskfree
member.param: [] The name of the volume on systems that support multiple disks or multiple memory cards. On single-volume systems, it is optional.
member.summary: Returns the free disk space.
member.remarks: The maximum size that can be supported 2048 GiB (2 terabyte).
member.returns: The amount of free space in KiB.

member.name: M:getchar
member.param: bool If true, the character read from the keyboard is echoed on the display.
member.summary: Read one character.
member.return: The numeric code for the character that is read (this is usually the ASCII code).

member.name: M:getstring
member.summary: Read a line.
member.return: The number of characters read, excluding the terminating null character.
member.remarks: Function getstring stops reading when either the enter key is typed or the maximum length is reached. The maximum length is in cells (not characters) and it includes a terminating null character. The function can read both packed and unpacked strings; when reading a packed string, the function may read more characters than the size parameter specifies, since each cell holds multiple characters.

member.name: M:getvalue
member.param: Must be between 2 and 36, use 10 for decimal or 16 for hexadecimal.
The character code that terminates the input. More than one character may be listed.
member.summary: Read a number.
member.remarks: Read a value (a signed number) from the keyboard. The getvalue function allows you to read in a numeric radix from 2 to 36 (the base parameter) with decimal radix by default. By default the input ends when the user types the enter key, but one or more different keys may be selected (the end parameter and subsequent). In the list of terminating keys, a positive number (like '\r') displays the key and terminates input, and a negative number terminates input without displaying the terminating key.

member.name: M:gotoxy
member.param: The new cursor position.
member.summary: Set cursor location.
member.return: true if the cursor moved and false if the requested position is invalid.
member.remarks: Sets the cursor position on the console. The upper left corner is at (1,1).

member.name: M:print
member.summary: Prints a string to the server console (not in-game chat) and logs (server_log.txt).
member.param: The string to print
The string to print

Colour codes for the foreground and the background of the text string; see function setattr for a list of colours. When left at -1, the default colours are used. Note that a terminal or a host application may not support colours.

member.name: M:random
member.param: The range of values (from 0 to max-1) that can be returned
member.summary: Get a pseudo-random number.
member.remarks: Using a value smaller than 1 gives weird values.

The standard random number generator of pawn is likely a linear congruential pseudo-random number generator with a range and a period of 2^31. Linear congruential pseudo-random number generators suffer from serial correlation (especially in the low bits) and it is unsuitable for applications that require high-quality random numbers.
member.returns: A random number ranging from 0 to max-1.

member.name: M:setattr
member.param: The string to print

Colour codes for the foreground and the background of the text string; see function setattr for a list of colours. When left at -1, the default colours are used. Note that a terminal or a host application may not support colours
member.summary: Set text colours.
member.remarks: On most systems, the colour value must be a value between zero and seven, as per the ANSI escape sequences, ISO 6429. Predefined constants for the colours are black (0), red (1), green (2), yellow (3), blue (4), magenta (5), cyan (6), and white (7).

member.name: M:setdate
member.param: The year to set; if set to "cellmin", the default value, it is ignored.
The month to set; if set to "cellmin", the default value, it is ignored.
The day to set; if set to "cellmin", the default value, it is ignored.
member.summary: Set the system date.
member.returns: This function always returns 0.
member.remarks: The date fields are kept in a valid range. For example, when setting the month to 13, it wraps back to 1 - this is always wrapped to the min/max, not modulo.

member.name: M:settime
member.param: The hour to set, in the range 0-23; if set to "cellmin", the default value, it is ignored.
The minute to set, in the range 0-59; if set to "cellmin", the default value, it is ignored.
The second to set, in the range 0-59; if set to "cellmin", the default value, it is ignored.
member.summary: Set the system time.
member.returns: This function always returns 0.
member.remarks: The time fields are kept in a valid range. For example, when setting the hour to 24, it wraps back to 0 - this is always wrapped to the min/max, not modulo.

member.name: M:settimer
member.summary: Configure the event timer.
member.param: The number of milliseconds to wait before calling the @timer callback function. If the timer is repetitive, this is the interval. When this parameter is 0 (zero), the timer is shut off.
If false, the timer is a repetitive timer; if true the timer is shut off after invoking the @timer event once.
member.remarks: See the chapter "Usage" for an example of this function, and the @timer event function.
member.returns: This function always returns 0.

member.name: M:settimestamp
member.summary: Sets the date and time with a single value.
member.param: The number of seconds that have elapsed since 00:00 hours, 1 January 1970. This particular date, 1 January 1970, is the UNIX system epoch.
member.returns: This function always returns 0.
member.remarks: The function getdate returns the number of seconds since 1 January 1970.

member.name: M:strcat
member.param: [] The string to store the two concatenated strings in
[] The source string
The maximum length of the destination (optional=sizeof (dest))
member.summary: This function concatenates (joins together) two strings into the destination string.
member.returns: The length of the new destination string.

member.name: M:strcmp
member.param: [] The first string to compare
[] The second string to compare
bool When set to true, the case doesn't matter - HeLLo is the same as Hello. When false, they're not the same (optional=0)
When this length is set, the first x chars will be compared - doing "Hello" and "Hell No" with a length of 4 will say it's the same string (optional=cellmax)
member.summary: Compares two strings to see if they are the same.
member.remarks: This function returns 0 if either string is empty. Check for null strings with isnull(). If you do not, for example, people can login to anyone's account by simply entering a blank password.
If you compare strings from a text file, you should take in to account the 'carriage return' and 'new line' special characters (\r \n), as they are included, when using fread.
member.returns:
0 if strings match each other on given length;.
1 or -1 if some character do not match: string1[i] - string2[i].
difference in number of characters if one string matches only part of another string.

member.name: M:strcopy
member.summary: Copies a string into the destination string.
member.param: The string to copy the source string into
The source string
The maximum length of the destination (optional=sizeof (dest))
member.returns: The length of the new destination string.

member.name: M:strequal
member.summary: Compares two strings to see if they are the same.
member.param: The first string to compare
The second string to compare
When set to true, the case doesn't matter - HeLLo is the same as Hello. When false, they're not the same (optional=0)
When this length is set, the first x chars will be compared - doing "Hello" and "Hell No" with a length of 4 will say it's the same string (optional=cellmax)
member.remarks: This is a conveniece function that depends on strcmp.
member.returns: true if the strings match each other on given length, false otherwise.

member.name: M:strfind
member.param: [] The string you want to search in (haystack)
[] The string you want to search for (needle)
bool When set to true, the case doesn't matter - HeLLo is the same as Hello. When false, they're not the same (optional=0)
The offset to start searching from (optional=0)
member.summary: Search for a sub string in a string.
member.returns: The number of characters before the sub string (the sub string's start position) or -1 if it's not found.

member.name: M:strlen
member.param: [] The string to get the length of
member.summary: Get the length of a string.
member.returns: The length of the string as an integer.

member.name: M:tickcount
member.param: & Upon return, this value contains the number of ticks that the internal system time will tick per second. This value therefore indicates the accuracy of the return value of this function (optional=0)
member.summary: This function can be used as a replacement for GetTickCount, as it returns the number of milliseconds since the start-up of the server.
member.returns: The number of milliseconds since start-up of the system. For a 32-bit cell, this count overflows after approximately 24 days of continuous operation.
